title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today's strongest trust-and-safety signal is methodological: several new papers focus on making AI systems more auditable, better evaluated, and more interpretable rather than simply more capable. The most relevant updates center on benchmark auditing, calibrated interpretability, verifiable agent environments, and architectures that emphasize persistent oversight and controllability.

items.title: Automated Benchmark Auditing for AI Agents and Large Language Models
items.why_it_matters: Benchmark auditing could catch hidden evaluation flaws before they distort safety and capability claims.

items.title: Confidence and Calibration of Activation Oracles for Reliable Interpretation of Language Model Internals
items.why_it_matters: Interpretability tools need calibrated uncertainty to support trustworthy safety analysis of model internals.

items.title: Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User's Digital World
items.why_it_matters: Evaluating assistants with broad digital access surfaces privacy, permissioning, and misuse risks earlier.

items.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
items.why_it_matters: It reframes agent safety around auditable execution layers, not just model-level improvements.

items.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
items.why_it_matters: Verifiable agent environments can improve reproducibility, red teaming, and safety evaluation at scale.

items.title: CausaLab: A Scalable Environment for Interactive Causal Discovery Toward AI Scientists
items.why_it_matters: It evaluates whether agents form correct causal hypotheses, not just plausible answers.

items.title: DiscoverPhysics: Benchmarking LLMs for Out-of-the-Box Scientific Thinking
items.why_it_matters: This helps separate genuine reasoning from memorization in frontier-model evaluations.

items.title: Peak-Then-Collapse and the Four Interface Channels of Knowledge-Graph Tool Use
items.why_it_matters: The reported collapse highlights instability in RL-based tool-use training for agents.

items.title: Rethinking Weak Supervision in Anomaly Detection: A Comprehensive Benchmark
items.why_it_matters: Better anomaly-detection benchmarks can strengthen monitoring and abuse-detection systems.

items.title: L2IR: Revealing Latent Intent in Graph Fraud Detection
items.why_it_matters: Fraud-detection advances matter for platform integrity and adversarial abuse prevention.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
